candidates.why: A separate operating-level depreciation line exists, but it is generic and not labelled as cost of materials / cost of sales / cost of revenues. Relative to the selected COGS bucket ('Cost of materials'), there is no clear evidence this depreciation specifically belongs to COGS, so adding it to COGS would risk misclassification.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Cost of materials'. The visible depreciation row is separate and unlabeled by function, so it should not be assigned to COGS absent explicit COGS wording.

candidates.why: A distinct depreciation line appears in the operating section at the same level as the selected overhead rows and outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Selling, General & Administrative Expenses', 'Salary and social expenses', 'Other operating expenses'). Because explicit separate D&A exists before EBIT, it appears not embedded in those selected rows and should be added separately once, using the summary depreciation row.

variables.notes: OK. Use the non-empty 'Depreciation' row with values, not the blank duplicate label. It is a separate operating expense line and is better matched to overhead/XSGA than to 'Cost of materials'.
